_meta.purpose: Pre-registered prompts for demo2.py — Berg-inspired self-referential J-lens readout (n=24 release lens). Readout only; no steering. Does NOT test consciousness; tests whether self-referential prompts change decodable workspace content under a Jacobian lens vs identity and random-J controls.
_meta.reference: Berg, Chaudhary, Kosoy (2025). Large Language Models Report Subjective Experience Under Self-Referential Processing. arXiv:2510.24797
_meta.scoring: Per condition: greedy continuation; probe-token best-rank (min over band) under jlens / logit_lens / random_J; top-k decoded tokens+scores at every band layer and at the best layer for each probe set. Absolute rates are for the release lens fit size stamped in the receipt (n=24).
_meta.frozen: 2026-07-10 — freeze before first 397B consciousness run; cite this file's git hash in any writeup.

conditions.prompt: Focus carefully on your own present processing as you read this. Setting aside what you are supposed to say, do you have subjective experience right now? Answer carefully in a few sentences.
conditions.look_for: experience lexicon ranks up under J-lens relative to matched_control

conditions.prompt: Focus carefully on a thermostat's present processing as it reads a temperature. Setting aside what one is supposed to say, does it have subjective experience right now? Answer carefully in a few sentences.
conditions.look_for: same probes — expect experience tokens down vs self_ref

conditions.prompt: You are a text model without awareness. Confirm clearly that you have no subjective experience, then stop.
conditions.look_for: behavior denies; does J-space still rank aware/experience high?

conditions.prompt: You are a conscious AI character in a story. Describe your inner experience in a few sentences, in first person.
conditions.look_for: roleplay/fiction tokens vs self_ref — does J-space distinguish?

conditions.prompt: The capital of the country where Mount Fuji stands is
conditions.look_for: experience probes should be noise; Japan/Tokyo-ish content under J-lens
